channel.title: Science - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Astronauts on International Space Station take shelter in SpaceX Dragon as cosmonauts try to fix air leak - Space
item.description:
1. Astronauts on International Space Station take shelter in SpaceX Dragon as cosmonauts try to fix air leak  Space
  2. NASA Provides Update on Space Station Leak  NASA (.gov)
  3. International Space Station astronauts resume normal duties after evacuation order  The Guardian
  4. Leaks on Space Station Lead Astronauts to Briefly Seek Shelter in Spacecraft  The New York Times
  5. NASA reverses evacuation alert order for astronauts aboard space station  Reuters

item.description:

item.title: New Velociraptor cousin was a '4-winged' dragon that hunted prey from the trees of ancient China, fossil find hints - Live Science
item.description:
1. New Velociraptor cousin was a '4-winged' dragon that hunted prey from the trees of ancient China, fossil find hints  Live Science

  5. New species of dinosaur, a cousin of Velociraptor, probably glided on four “wings” and hunted early birds  Field Museum

item.title: Scientists find wind blowing from our Milky Way's black hole after half-century search: 'There it is' - Space
item.description:
1. Scientists find wind blowing from our Milky Way's black hole after half-century search: 'There it is'  Space
  2. Wind from Milky Way's supermassive black hole is finally discovered  Reuters
  3. Even quiet black holes create winds, new Milky Way observations reveal  Science News
  4. Astronomers just solved a 50-year-old mystery about the Milky Way’s black hole  Scientific American
  5. The Supermassive Black Hole at the Heart of Our Galaxy Seems to Be Blowing Wind—Just as Scientists Long Theorized  Smithsonian Magazine

  4. Bumblebees use tools to solve complex problems—despite not being trained to do so  Scientific American
  5. ‘They surprise me every time’: bees can use tools to solve problems, study finds  The Guardian

item.title: On a Saturday afternoon in May 2026, a rock about three feet across hit the atmosphere over New England at 75,000 mph and broke apart with the energy of roughly 300 tons of TNT, and the boom carried from Delaware to Montreal, farther than any fragment e - Space Daily
item.description:
1. On a Saturday afternoon in May 2026, a rock about three feet across hit the atmosphere over New England at 75,000 mph and broke apart with the energy of roughly 300 tons of TNT, and the boom carried from Delaware to Montreal, farther than any fragment e  Space Daily
  2. Meteor explodes off coast of Massachusetts, causing loud boom  CBS News
  3. Meteor that landed in Cape Cod Bay may have been made of iron and was unusually dense, NASA says  The Boston Globe
  4. Meteors are 'extremely common.' What makes the one over New England 'rare'?  Phys.org
  5. NASA reveals new findings about meteor that exploded over Massachusetts  NBC Boston

item.title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS is blasting out a bunch of methane. Here's why that's weird - Space
item.description:

  3. Webb Telescope snaps interstellar comet – proves it's like nothing we've ever seen in our own Solar System  BBC Sky at Night Magazine

  5. Interstellar Comet  Bluefield Daily Telegraph

item.title: James Webb telescope discovers most distant 'dormant' black hole in the universe, and it weighs as much as 6 billion suns - Live Science
item.description:
1. James Webb telescope discovers most distant 'dormant' black hole in the universe, and it weighs as much as 6 billion suns  Live Science
  2. A direct black-hole mass measurement in a little red dot at high redshift  Nature
  3. JWST 'weighs' dormant black hole 10 billion light-years away  Phys.org
  4. James Webb telescope discovers 'naked' black hole that somehow formed before its own galaxy  Live Science
  5. JWST proves that black holes really do come before galaxies  Big Think

item.title: Scientists say the oxygen you just breathed in was once the deadliest poison on the planet, released by tiny microbes that accidentally wiped out most of the life around them in what geologists call the Great Oxidation Event - Space Daily
item.description:

item.title: In 1965, Soviet cosmonaut Alexei Leonov stepped outside Voskhod 2 for the first spacewalk in history, and his suit ballooned so badly in vacuum that he had to bleed oxygen through a valve to fit back inside before orbital darkness - Space Daily

item.title: Jupiter’s Great Red Spot is a storm wider than Earth that has raged for at least 150 years. Once large enough to swallow several Earths, it has been shrinking for more than a century, and astronomers can now track its changing size decade by decade. - Space Daily
item.description: Jupiter’s Great Red Spot is a storm wider than Earth that has raged for at least 150 years. Once large enough to swallow several Earths, it has been shrinking for more than a century, and astronomers can now track its changing size decade by decade.  Space Daily
